mini_title: Organic Synthesis: Organic Chemistry Highlights: Recent Adva
brief_summary: This issue of Organic Chemistry Highlights summarizes recent advances in organic synthesis, targeting researchers in the field. It covers new methodologies and catalytic reactions, including alcohol-alcohol cross-coupling and chromoselective photocatalysis. A significant portion details enantioselective synthesis techniques for quaternary stereocenters and hydrofluorination.

detailed_summary.issue_thesis: This issue summarizes recent cutting-edge research in organic synthesis, focusing on novel methodologies, enantioselective transformations, and complex total syntheses.

sections.heading: New Methodologies & Catalysis
bullets: A nickel-mediated S H 2 radical sorting process enables alcohol-alcohol cross-coupling to form C(sp³)–C(sp³) bonds via deoxygenation in a single vessel, tolerant of air (MacMillan, Science).

A nickel-catalyzed platform achieves stereodivergent, kinetically controlled E/Z-selective synthesis of internal alkenes from terminal alkenes via one-carbon transposition at room temperature (Engle/Vantourout, Angew. Chem. Int. Ed.).

An organic helicenium photocatalyst (n-Pr-DMQA+) enables wavelength-dependent activation of C(sp²)–X bonds, allowing selective activation of C(sp²)–I bonds over C(sp²)–Br bonds in iodobromoarenes (Gianetti, JACS).

A proposed Rh(I)-catalyzed cascade synthesizes substituted seven-membered oxacycles from cyclobutenol derivatives, involving C–C bond formation and subsequent C(sp³)–C(sp³) bond cleavage (Sarpong/Baik, J. Org. Chem.).

A metallaphotoredox method couples aryl bromides with benzyl bromides to form di(hetero)arylmethanes, optimized using high-throughput experimentation (HTE) and shown to be scalable (Rauch, Org. Lett.).

sections.heading: Enantioselective Synthesis
bullets: A cooperative photoredox/Fe/chiral primary amine triple catalysis system constructs all-carbon quaternary stereocenters by coupling unactivated alkyl sources with tertiary alkyl moieties under mild, base-free conditions (Yang, JACS).

An enantioselective Cu(I)-catalyzed propargylic substitution of propargylic carbonates produces α-tertiary ethynylamines, ethers, and all-carbon quaternary centers using N-, O-, and C-centered nucleophiles (Zhou/Wang, Nat. Chem.).

Regio- and enantioselective C–F bond formation on unactivated terminal and internal alkenes is achieved using NiH catalysis with a tailored chiral Bn-BOx ligand, creating aliphatic C–F stereogenic centers (Hong, JACS).

A copper-catalyzed enantioconvergent radical N-alkylation couples diverse (hetero)aromatic amines with secondary/tertiary alkyl halides, effective for sterically congested nucleophiles via an outer-sphere mechanism (Liu/Yu/Li, JACS).

A bifunctional chiral phosphoric acid photosensitizer catalyzes a highly enantioselective [2π + 2σ] photocycloaddition of bicyclo[1.1.0]butanes with vinylazaarenes, yielding pharmaceutically relevant bicyclo[2.1.1]hexane derivatives (Jiang, JACS).

sections.heading: Late-Stage Functionalization & Total Synthesis
bullets: The enantioselective, divergent synthesis of 10 diterpenoid pyrones was achieved in 14–16 steps from chiral pool enoxolone, including the first total syntheses of higginsianins A, B, D, and E (Li, JACS).

A photocatalytic hydroarylation functionalizes dehydroalanine (Dha) residues in peptides using arylthianthrenium salts, enabling late-stage synthesis of unconventional phenylalanine derivatives (Noel, Angew. Chem. Int. Ed.).

The first total synthesis of pallamolides A–E was completed, featuring diastereoselective sequential Michael additions, SmI₂-mediated cyclization/reduction, and an acid-mediated cascade (Jia, Angew. Chem. Int. Ed.).

The first total synthesis of the Euphorbia diterpenoid pepluacetal was achieved in racemic and chiral forms, featuring a photo-induced Wolff rearrangement/lactonization cascade and a Rh-catalyzed transannular C(sp³)–H carbenoid insertion (She, Angew. Chem. Int. Ed.).

A (3+2) cycloaddition of in situ-generated difluoromethyl nitrile oxide with alkenes enables late-stage difluoromethylation of finasteride and carbamazepine, with resulting CF₂H-isoxazolines convertible to various scaffolds (Mykhailiuk, Org. Lett.).

structured_payload.mini_title: Organic Synthesis: Organic Chemistry Highlights: Recent Advances in Synthesis
structured_payload.brief_summary: This issue of Organic Chemistry Highlights summarizes recent advances in organic synthesis, targeting researchers in the field. It covers new methodologies and catalytic reactions, including alcohol-alcohol cross-coupling and chromoselective photocatalysis. A significant portion details enantioselective synthesis techniques for quaternary stereocenters and hydrofluorination. The summary also highlights late-stage functionalization and total synthesis efforts for complex natural products and peptides. No explicit call to action is provided.
